reference: Physical constraint: primary tubing hanger seal (metal-to-metal in a CCS well) sits at the interface between the tubing string and the wellhead bore — primary seal failure requires pulling the tubing string to access and replace the seal assembly; secondary seal re-energisation via wellhead tooling is a temporary workaround not a repair of the primary barrier; analogous to control_line logic
notes: Corrected from light_intervention to full_workover; light intervention only valid for secondary seal activation (workaround); restoring primary barrier integrity requires full tubing pull; safety_critical=true and can_defer=false retained — tubing hanger primary seal failure is a safety-barrier event requiring immediate response

reference: OREDA Handbook (all editions) — data collected from offshore HC production and injection wells in North Sea / Gulf of Mexico / SE Asia
notes: CRITICAL LIMITATION: OREDA covers hydrocarbon service only. CO2 injection is a fundamentally different service environment on three dimensions: (1) Corrosion — supercritical CO2 + formation brine = carbonic acid; HC service corrosion rates do not translate directly; (2) Elastomer compatibility — Nitrile/HNBR degrade in CO2; Aflas/PEEK required; (3) Thermal cycling — CO2 injection temperature vs reservoir temperature creates fatigue cycles absent in HC production. Where OREDA analogues are used in this register they represent the best available starting point only — they should be progressively replaced by CCS-specific field observations as the industry matures. No equivalent of OREDA exists for CCS wells as of 2026.

reference: Field calibration engine — FIELD_C (Quest analogue; Canada; 4 injectors; 9 years of operation): 2 tubing failures observed vs 1.0 expected from P10_MTTF=35yr assumption
notes: First field calibration data point confirming tubing MTTF assumption is optimistic; observed failure rate is 2× model expectation at 10% confidence (2 events); effective calibration factor = 1.10 (10% MTTF reduction) at current confidence level; as more events accumulate confidence will increase and calibration factor influence will grow; this is the expected outcome when transferring HC-service OREDA analogues to CO2 injection service without elastomer/corrosion adjustment

reference: Hardiman et al. 2023 "Deep Geological Storage of CO2 on the UK Continental Shelf: Containment Certainty — Supplementary Note C: Well Analysis Using Peloton WellMaster Database" — WSP / Crondall Energy / GeoEnergy Durham for NSTA (February 2023). Peloton WellMaster: 38yr dataset; 6000 wells; 70000 components; 45000 svc-yr; 34 operators; includes Norwegian CO2 injection wells; filtered post-1996 UK+Norway injectors
notes: Component-level AFR from WellMaster (post-1996 UK+Norway injectors unless noted): SCSSV/TRSV AFR=0.009/yr → MTTF≈111yr (3549 svc-yr); injection packer AFR=0.002/yr → MTTF≈500yr (15263 svc-yr); tubing AFR=0.004/yr per 1000m (7993 svc-yr); cement sheath AFR=0.001/yr (DERIVED as half of packer — not empirical); subsea XmasTree AFR=0.007/yr → MTTF≈143yr (563 svc-yr); injection master valve AFR=0.003/yr → MTTF≈333yr (1624 svc-yr); annulus master valve AFR=0.002/yr → MTTF≈500yr (1468 svc-yr); subsea tubing hanger: 0 failures in 13026 svc-yr; subsea wellhead: 0 failures in 5372 svc-yr; casing AFR=0.004/yr per 1000m (15506 svc-yr). Simulated barrier failure rates for 25yr CO2 injector: primary=0.034/yr; secondary=0.040/yr → combined well leak risk=0.0020/yr (1 in 500 per year). All AFRs from HC-service data with CO2 injection analogue filtering; CO2 elastomer/corrosion effects not separately modelled — introduces optimistic bias for CO2-specific failure modes.

reference: No single authoritative source; reflects consensus across IEAGHG 2018-08; SPE-232388-MS (2026); Hardiman et al. 2023 Note C; NZTC/DNV CCS Wells Technology Roadmap (2025); OREDA Handbook
notes: CRITICAL DISCLAIMER: CCS well integrity is a relatively new discipline with limited field data. As of 2026 the global CCS injection fleet numbers fewer than 50 commercial-scale wells with CO2 injection history spanning at most ~30 years (Sleipner 1996). No CCS-specific equivalent of OREDA exists. No publicly available CCS-only MTTF database exists. All MTTF values in this register are HC-service analogues adjusted by expert judgement or drawn from pilot-scale operational experience. The calibration score of ~38/100 (Pre-FEED) accurately reflects this data maturity. Assumptions should be treated as working estimates subject to revision as: (1) field calibration accumulates (FIELD_C and future fields); (2) CCS-specific reliability databases emerge; (3) longer operational histories become available from Sleipner / Quest / Northern Lights / Boundary Dam. Users should not interpret low-confidence MTTF values as validated engineering data — they are structured expert judgement with documented uncertainty bounds.

reference: SPE-232388-MS (Magalhaes & Graterol; SLB; presented Oman OPES May 2026). Page 5: 'The MTTF values utilized in this study are for the sole purpose of validating the model and testing the stochastic framework. Despite best efforts to use representative parameters these values are illustrative only and should not be used as references or design bases for specific CCS projects.' Page 3: infant mortality and wear-out phases explicitly excluded — SPE model assumes constant failure rate (useful life only).
notes: CRITICAL: SPE-232388-MS Table 1 values (TRSV P10=30/P90=65yr; Packer P10=22/P90=38yr; Tubing P10=35/P90=55yr) are (1) explicitly illustrative per the authors and (2) calibrated for a constant-failure-rate model with no bathtub curve — SPE assumes commissioning eliminates infant mortality and MMV surveillance catches wear-out before functional failure. This tool applies a bathtub curve multiplier (1.5x infant mortality; 1.0x useful life; up to 1.8x wear-out) on top of those useful-life-only values introducing a systematic conservative bias of approximately 6% over a 30-year horizon relative to SPE's own predictions with the same inputs. Peloton WellMaster AFR values (Hardiman et al. 2023 Note C) are lifecycle-averaged (observed failures / total service-years across all phases) and are a more coherent input for a bathtub-curve model. SPE Table 1 values are retained as secondary cross-checks and for CO2-specific failure modes (particularly packer elastomers) where WellMaster HC-service data is known to be optimistic — but should not be cited as validated CCS field reliability data.
